content:
Audit this FINAL 4.5-MONTH FORENSIC EXPO GO → ANDROID NATIVE MIGRATION AUDIT

This is not another route-count report.

The IVX application has approximately 4.5 months of development history.
We must prove that every real feature, screen, workflow, service, database path,
owner tool, admin function, AI capability, CRM module, and business function
created during that period was migrated from the Expo Go application into the
current Android-native production application.

Do not declare migration complete based only on:

- number of current routes;
- number of current files;
- successful compilation;
- current Android navigation;
- static code scanning;
- build-marker changes;
- a list generated from the latest branch.

The audit must compare the full historical Expo application against the final
Android-native application.

PRIMARY OBJECTIVE

1. Reconstruct the complete 4.5-month IVX feature history.
2. Identify every feature that ever existed in Expo Go.
3. Identify every feature that exists only in old branches, commits, repos,
   local Rork workspace, screenshots, builds, or deleted files.
4. Map every historical feature to the current Android-native equivalent.
5. Restore every valid missing capability.
6. Remove obsolete or unsafe legacy behavior.
7. Build, install, deploy, and verify the final native application.
8. Return hard evidence proving no valid IVX functionality was lost.

==================================================
PHASE 1 — FREEZE CURRENT PRODUCTION
==================================================

Before modifying code:

1. Record current production GitHub SHA.
2. Record current Render deployed SHA.
3. Record Android build SHA.
4. Record web build SHA.
5. Record production API URL.
6. Record production Supabase project.
7. Create a database backup.
8. Create a repository archive.
9. Export current route inventory.
10. Export current module inventory.
11. Do not delete historical files, branches, tags, artifacts, or data.
12. Do not overwrite production without a rollback point.

Return:

CURRENT_REPOSITORY=
CURRENT_BRANCH=
CURRENT_HEAD_SHA=
CURRENT_RENDER_SHA=
CURRENT_ANDROID_SHA=
CURRENT_WEB_SHA=
CURRENT_SUPABASE_PROJECT=
BACKUP_LOCATION=
ROLLBACK_SHA=

==================================================
PHASE 2 — DISCOVER EVERY IVX CODE SOURCE
==================================================

Audit every repository and code source used during the last 4.5 months.

Known repositories may include:

- ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest
- ibb142/Ivxholding-2
- ibb142/rork-testing-app
- ibb142/rork-ivxholding--1
- any additional IVX repo, fork, branch, mirror, export, or archive

Search:

- all GitHub repositories
- all branches
- all tags
- all releases
- deleted branches recoverable from history
- pull requests
- GitHub Actions artifacts
- Render deployment sources
- Vercel deployment sources
- EAS build sources
- Android build artifacts
- Rork workspace history
- downloaded ZIP files
- backup archives
- local generated code
- old APKs
- old web bundles
- old Expo bundles
- screenshots showing features no longer present

For every source return:

SOURCE
REPOSITORY
BRANCH
FIRST_COMMIT
LAST_COMMIT
COMMIT_COUNT
FEATURES_FOUND
DEPLOYED_BEFORE
CURRENTLY_MERGED
STATUS

==================================================
PHASE 3 — BUILD THE 4.5-MONTH COMMIT TIMELINE
==================================================

Create a chronological history from the first IVX commit to today.

For every meaningful commit return:

DATE
REPOSITORY
BRANCH
COMMIT_SHA
COMMIT_MESSAGE
FILES_CHANGED
FEATURES_ADDED
FEATURES_REMOVED
MIGRATIONS
API_CHANGES
DATABASE_CHANGES
DEPLOYED=true/false
DEPLOY_TARGET
CURRENT_NATIVE_EQUIVALENT
STATUS

Classify every commit:

- PRESENT_IN_NATIVE
- PARTIALLY_MIGRATED
- MISSING_FROM_NATIVE
- OBSOLETE
- SECURITY_RISK
- DUPLICATE
- NEVER_DEPLOYED
- DEPLOYED_THEN_LOST

Do not ignore commits because they are old.

==================================================
PHASE 4 — RECONSTRUCT THE COMPLETE EXPO APPLICATION
==================================================

Check out the last known working Expo versions and reconstruct the complete
historical Expo application.

Inventory every:

- route
- tab
- stack
- modal
- drawer
- screen
- component
- hook
- service
- API client
- database query
- storage bucket
- realtime subscription
- background task
- notification
- authentication flow
- owner/admin flow
- CRM flow
- AI flow
- media flow
- financial flow
- registration flow

Search all Expo-era files, including deleted and renamed files.

Required Expo inventory fields:

EXPO_FEATURE_ID
FEATURE_NAME
ROUTE
FILE
COMPONENT
SERVICE
API_ENDPOINT
DATABASE_TABLE
STORAGE
AUTH_REQUIREMENT
ROLE_REQUIREMENT
FIRST_COMMIT
LAST_WORKING_COMMIT
SCREENSHOT_AVAILABLE
PRODUCTION_USED
STATUS

==================================================
PHASE 5 — RECONSTRUCT THE COMPLETE NATIVE APP
==================================================

Inventory the current Android-native app independently.

Do not copy the Expo inventory and assume equivalence.

For every native capability return:

NATIVE_FEATURE_ID
FEATURE_NAME
ANDROID_ROUTE
SOURCE_FILE
NATIVE_COMPONENT
SERVICE
API_ENDPOINT
DATABASE_TABLE
AUTH_REQUIREMENT
ROLE_REQUIREMENT
BUILD_SHA
DEVICE_TEST_RESULT
STATUS

==================================================
PHASE 6 — EXPO-TO-NATIVE MIGRATION MATRIX
==================================================

Create a one-to-one migration matrix.

Required columns:

FEATURE_ID
FEATURE_NAME
EXPO_ROUTE
EXPO_FILE
EXPO_LAST_WORKING_SHA
ANDROID_ROUTE
ANDROID_FILE
ANDROID_BUILD_SHA
SAME_FUNCTIONALITY
SAME_DATA_SOURCE
SAME_PERMISSIONS
SAME_ACTIONS
SAME_MEDIA_SUPPORT
SAME_PERSISTENCE
DEVICE_VERIFIED
WEB_VERIFIED
MIGRATION_STATUS
EVIDENCE

Allowed migration statuses:

- VERIFIED_NATIVE_EQUIVALENT
- MIGRATED_WITH_IMPROVEMENT
- PARTIALLY_MIGRATED
- MISSING_FROM_NATIVE
- EXPO_ONLY
- REMOVED_INTENTIONALLY
- OBSOLETE
- BLOCKED

Every EXPO_ONLY, PARTIALLY_MIGRATED, or MISSING_FROM_NATIVE item must be fixed
or formally documented with owner-approved removal.

==================================================
PHASE 7 — AUDIT EVERY SCREEN
==================================================

Compare every historical Expo screen with the Android-native replacement.

Verify:

1. Screen exists.
2. Screen opens.
3. Screen design is complete.
4. Navigation works.
5. Back button works.
6. Deep link works where applicable.
7. Authentication works.
8. Role restrictions work.
9. Data loads.
10. Create works.
11. Edit works.
12. Delete works where allowed.
13. Search works.
14. Filter works.
15. Pagination works.
16. Upload works.
17. Empty state is honest.
18. Error state is honest.
19. Restart preserves data.
20. No Expo Go dependency remains.

Do not mark a screen migrated because a placeholder route exists.

==================================================
PHASE 8 — AUDIT EVERY BUSINESS MODULE
==================================================

Compare historical Expo and current native functionality for:

CORE APP

- Home
- Invest
- Market
- Portfolio
- Chat
- Profile
- Search
- Notifications
- Settings
- Language
- Security
- Support
- Legal

AUTHENTICATION

- signup
- login
- owner login
- admin login
- email verification
- phone verification
- password recovery
- owner recovery
- session restore
- logout
- role resolution

OWNER AND ADMIN

- Admin Panel
- Admin HQ
- Owner Console
- Owner Controls
- Access Control
- Users
- Members
- Staff
- Roles
- Permissions
- Screen Access
- Data Scopes
- Audit Trail
- Variables
- Security Box
- API Keys Vault
- Deployment Control
- GitHub Control
- Render Control
- Diagnostics
- Data Recovery
- Backups

USERS AND ROLES

- Members
- Investors
- Buyers
- JV Partners
- Influencers
- Realtors
- Brokers
- Agents
- Tokenized Investors
- Lenders
- IVX Staff
- Admins
- Auditors

PROPERTY AND INVESTMENT

- properties
- property detail
- featured properties
- live deals
- JV deals
- tokenized deals
- ownership shares
- holdings
- investments
- portfolio
- statements
- returns
- distributions
- exits
- documents
- title
- permits
- insurance
- media

CRM

- investor CRM
- buyer CRM
- realtor CRM
- broker CRM
- influencer CRM
- lender CRM
- contacts
- leads
- outreach
- campaigns
- matching
- scoring
- pipeline
- opportunities
- deal tracking

FINANCE

- wallets
- deposits
- withdrawals
- transactions
- revenue
- fees
- investor payments
- realtor commissions
- influencer commissions
- broker commissions
- statements
- reports
- audit

AI

- IVX Owner AI
- Developer Workspace
- Review
- Debug
- Plan
- Patch
- Analyze
- AI Outreach
- AI Video
- AI Gallery
- AI Reports
- Lead Intelligence
- Global Intelligence
- Opportunity Scanner
- Document Summary
- Deal Review
- Investor Reply
- Voice
- File Analysis
- Image Analysis
- PDF Analysis
- Live Work
- Watchdog
- Trace Logs

COMMUNICATIONS

- chat
- direct messages
- rooms
- email
- inbox
- SMS
- push notifications
- broadcast
- social command
- campaign messaging

MEDIA

- image upload
- video upload
- PDF upload
- document upload
- gallery
- reels
- likes
- comments
- shares
- thumbnails
- playback
- compression
- storage

For every module return:

EXPO_CAPABILITY_COUNT
NATIVE_CAPABILITY_COUNT
MATCHED
PARTIAL
MISSING
REMOVED
DEVICE_VERIFIED
FINAL_STATUS

==================================================
PHASE 9 — AUDIT DATABASE AND DATA PATHS
==================================================

A screen is not migrated if it points to the wrong database or table.

Compare Expo and Android data paths for:

- auth.users
- profiles
- members
- investors
- buyers
- partners
- lenders
- staff
- roles
- permissions
- properties
- deals
- JV deals
- holdings
- transactions
- wallets
- revenue
- fees
- registrations
- waitlist
- landing submissions
- conversations
- messages
- notifications
- CRM
- campaigns
- leads
- matches
- audit logs
- variables

For every historical query return:

EXPO_FILE
EXPO_QUERY
EXPO_PROJECT
EXPO_TABLE
ANDROID_FILE
ANDROID_QUERY
ANDROID_PROJECT
ANDROID_TABLE
DATA_EQUIVALENT
COUNT_MATCH
STATUS

Identify:

- queries removed during migration;
- queries pointing to old projects;
- queries replaced by empty placeholders;
- tables never created;
- local-cache-only functionality;
- RLS mismatches;
- hardcoded zero counts;
- legacy data no longer visible.

==================================================
PHASE 10 — AUDIT EVERY API
==================================================

Compare every Expo API call with the native implementation.

For every API:

METHOD
EXPO_ROUTE
EXPO_CALLER
ANDROID_ROUTE
ANDROID_CALLER
BACKEND_HANDLER
AUTH
PERMISSION
DATABASE
LIVE_HTTP_STATUS
RESPONSE_SCHEMA_MATCH
STATUS

Find:

- missing native calls;
- Expo-only endpoints;
- dev-only routes still needed in production;
- renamed endpoints not updated;
- wrong base URLs;
- stale Render services;
- local fallback APIs;
- mock responses;
- fake success responses.

==================================================
PHASE 11 — AUDIT LOCAL AND OFFLINE FEATURES
==================================================

Search historical Expo code for:

- AsyncStorage
- SecureStore
- SQLite
- MMKV
- cached JSON
- offline queues
- draft messages
- local member registry
- saved filters
- media drafts
- upload queues
- notification state
- owner session cache
- feature flags

For every historical local feature determine:

- migrated to native storage;
- moved to server persistence;
- intentionally removed;
- accidentally lost.

Return:

LOCAL_FEATURE
EXPO_STORAGE_KEY
EXPO_FILE
NATIVE_REPLACEMENT
MIGRATED
DATA_RECOVERED
STATUS

==================================================
PHASE 12 — AUDIT EXPO-ONLY DEPENDENCIES
==================================================

Search all current production code for:

- Expo Go
- exp://
- Metro dependency
- tunnel dependency
- appOwnership
- executionEnvironment
- Expo Go owner gate
- Rork runtime
- Rork API
- Rork AI Cloud
- localhost
- local fallback
- dev-only production behavior
- mock native modules

Classify every match:

FILE
LINE
PURPOSE
PRODUCTION_REACHABLE
ACTION
FINAL_STATUS

No production-critical feature may still require Expo Go.

==================================================
PHASE 13 — FIND FEATURES LOST DURING REWRITES
==================================================

Use semantic and AST-level comparison between historical Expo code and current
native code.

Search for capabilities that disappeared because:

- files were renamed;
- routes were replaced;
- components were simplified;
- large screens were rewritten with fewer actions;
- backend calls were removed;
- UI buttons remain but handlers were deleted;
- old schemas were abandoned;
- Rork generated a smaller replacement;
- migration preserved appearance but not behavior.

For every lost capability return:

FEATURE
OLD_FILE
OLD_COMMIT
OLD_BEHAVIOR
CURRENT_FILE
CURRENT_BEHAVIOR
LOSS_CONFIRMED
BUSINESS_IMPACT
FIX_REQUIRED

==================================================
PHASE 14 — SCREENSHOT-TO-CODE AUDIT
==================================================

Use all available historical screenshots as evidence.

For every screenshot:

1. Identify screen.
2. Identify approximate date.
3. Identify likely commit/build.
4. List visible functions.
5. Compare with current Android screen.
6. Mark missing buttons, modules, counts, menus, and actions.
7. Trace missing functions to historical code.
8. Restore valid missing functions.

Return:

SCREENSHOT_ID
SCREEN
DATE
VISIBLE_FEATURES
CURRENT_MATCH
MISSING_FEATURES
RECOVERY_ACTION
STATUS

==================================================
PHASE 15 — RESTORE EVERY VALID MISSING FEATURE
==================================================

For every item marked:

- PARTIALLY_MIGRATED
- MISSING_FROM_NATIVE
- EXPO_ONLY
- DEPLOYED_THEN_LOST

Perform:

1. Review historical implementation.
2. Validate business requirement.
3. Validate current schema compatibility.
4. Port functionality to native architecture.
5. Add backend support.
6. Add database migration if required.
7. Add permissions.
8. Add tests.
9. Add error handling.
10. Add persistence.
11. Test Android.
12. Test web where applicable.
13. Commit.
14. Deploy.
15. Verify live.

Do not copy unsafe old code blindly.

==================================================
PHASE 16 — CLEAN NATIVE BUILD
==================================================

Perform a clean release build:

1. Delete node_modules.
2. Delete build outputs.
3. Delete project Gradle cache.
4. Reinstall from lockfile.
5. Run typecheck.
6. Run lint.
7. Run unit tests.
8. Run integration tests.
9. Run API tests.
10. Run database tests.
11. Run permission tests.
12. Run Android tests.
13. Run Gradle clean.
14. Build debug APK.
15. Build signed release APK.
16. Build signed AAB.
17. Install release APK on a clean Android device.
18. Launch without Expo Go.
19. Launch without Metro.
20. Launch with development computer disconnected.

Return command, exit code, duration, and logs.

==================================================
PHASE 17 — FULL CLEAN-DEVICE TEST
==================================================

On a clean Android device:

1. Install the signed release APK.
2. Launch from Android launcher.
3. Verify package name.
4. Verify version.
5. Verify build SHA.
6. Sign in as owner.
7. Restart app.
8. Confirm owner session.
9. Open every primary tab.
10. Open every owner/admin section.
11. Open every user/role module.
12. Open every CRM module.
13. Open every investment module.
14. Open every finance module.
15. Open every AI module.
16. Open every media module.
17. Create test data.
18. Edit test data.
19. Delete test data where allowed.
20. Upload image.
21. Upload video.
22. Upload PDF.
23. Send chat messages.
24. Receive AI response.
25. Test notifications.
26. Test offline/reconnect.
27. Restart app.
28. Confirm persistence.
29. Logout.
30. Login again.
31. Confirm data remains.

Provide continuous video proof.

==================================================
PHASE 18 — PRODUCTION DEPLOYMENT
==================================================

After all missing capabilities are restored:

1. Merge into the owner-controlled repository.
2. Push to the production branch.
3. Trigger Render deployment.
4. Deploy web.
5. Build Android from the same commit.
6. Wait for LIVE.
7. Verify /health HTTP 200.
8. Verify commit marker.
9. Verify database.
10. Verify owner auth.
11. Verify module APIs.
12. Verify native app.

Required equality:

GITHUB_HEAD_SHA
=
RENDER_DEPLOYED_SHA
=
BACKEND_COMMIT_MARKER
=
ANDROID_BUILD_SHA
=
WEB_BUILD_SHA

==================================================
PHASE 19 — FINAL MIGRATION REPORT
==================================================

Return exact totals:

HISTORICAL_REPOSITORIES_FOUND=
HISTORICAL_BRANCHES_FOUND=
HISTORICAL_COMMITS_AUDITED=
EXPO_SCREENS_FOUND=
EXPO_SERVICES_FOUND=
EXPO_API_CALLS_FOUND=
EXPO_FEATURES_FOUND=
ANDROID_FEATURES_FOUND=
VERIFIED_NATIVE_EQUIVALENTS=
PARTIALLY_MIGRATED=
MISSING_FROM_NATIVE=
RESTORED=
REMOVED_INTENTIONALLY=
OBSOLETE=
BLOCKED=
MIGRATION_COMPLETION_PERCENT=

The percentage must be calculated from the historical migration matrix.

Do not use only the current 224-screen count.

==================================================
PHASE 20 — REQUIRED EVIDENCE PACKAGE
==================================================

SOURCE HISTORY

- every repository
- every branch
- first commit
- last commit
- historical feature count
- undeployed commits
- lost commits
- restored commits

MIGRATION MATRIX

- every Expo feature
- native equivalent
- status
- evidence
- device result

DATABASE

- historical project references
- current project
- table mappings
- lost queries
- restored queries
- count reconciliation

DEPLOYMENT

- final repository
- final branch
- final SHA
- Render deploy ID
- Render SHA
- Android SHA
- web SHA
- health result

ANDROID

- APK
- AAB
- versionName
- versionCode
- package name
- signing fingerprint
- clean-device result
- no Expo Go result
- no Metro result

==================================================
FINAL VERDICT
==================================================

Return exactly one:

FULL 4.5-MONTH EXPO-TO-ANDROID MIGRATION VERIFIED

or

FULL MIGRATION NOT VERIFIED

Do not return VERIFIED unless:

- every historical repo and branch was searched;
- every Expo-era feature was inventoried;
- every historical feature was mapped;
- every missing valid feature was restored;
- every intentional removal was documented;
- every database path was reconciled;
- every API was compared;
- every critical module passed on a clean device;
- signed APK and AAB were produced;
- GitHub, Render, Android, and web SHAs match;
- no Expo Go dependency remains;
- evidence is complete.

If incomplete, return:

HISTORICAL_SOURCES_NOT_SEARCHED:
EXPO_FEATURES_FOUND:
NATIVE_EQUIVALENTS:
PARTIAL:
MISSING:
RESTORED:
BLOCKED:
UNDEPLOYED_COMMITS:
WRONG_REPOSITORIES:
WRONG_BRANCHES:
DATABASE_MISMATCHES:
NEXT_REQUIRED_ACTION:

Do not give another route-count-only report.
Audit the complete 4.5-month product history, restore all valid missing code,
build the signed native app, deploy it, and prove the migration end-to-end.
